section: Chapter 4: Linear Equations in Two Variables

questions.question: What is a linear equation in two variables?
questions.correct_answer: An equation that can be written in the form ax + by + c = 0 where a and b are not both zero
questions.explanation: It represents a relationship between two variables x and y.

questions.question: What is the general form of a linear equation in two variables?
questions.explanation: Here a, b and c are real numbers and a and b cannot both be zero.

questions.question: What is meant by a solution of a linear equation in two variables?
questions.correct_answer: An ordered pair (x, y) that satisfies the equation
questions.explanation: Substituting the values of x and y makes the equation true.

questions.question: How are solutions of equations in two variables written?
questions.explanation: The first value represents x and the second represents y.

questions.question: How many solutions does a linear equation in two variables have?
questions.explanation: Different combinations of x and y can satisfy the same equation.

questions.question: How can we check whether an ordered pair is a solution?
questions.correct_answer: Substitute the values of x and y into the equation
questions.explanation: If both sides of the equation are equal, the pair is a solution.

questions.question: What type of graph represents a linear equation in two variables?
questions.explanation: All solution points of the equation lie on this straight line.

questions.question: What does each point on the graph of a linear equation represent?
questions.explanation: Every point on the line satisfies the equation.

questions.question: What happens when x = 0 is substituted into a linear equation?
questions.correct_answer: We get the corresponding value of y
questions.explanation: This helps find one solution of the equation easily.

questions.question: What happens when y = 0 is substituted into a linear equation?
questions.correct_answer: We get the corresponding value of x
questions.explanation: This provides another solution of the equation.

notes.title: Linear Equation Form
notes.content: A linear equation in two variables can always be written in the form ax + by + c = 0.

notes.title: Solutions as Ordered Pairs
notes.content: Solutions of equations in two variables are written as ordered pairs (x, y).

notes.title: Infinite Solutions
notes.content: A linear equation in two variables has infinitely many solutions.

notes.title: Graph Representation
notes.content: The graph of a linear equation in two variables is a straight line.

notes.title: Solution Verification
notes.content: Substituting values of x and y into the equation helps verify whether a pair is a solution.

facts.fact: Every solution of a linear equation corresponds to a point on its graph.
Every point on the graph of a linear equation satisfies the equation.
Linear equations represent relationships between two quantities.
Different ordered pairs can satisfy the same linear equation.
